sections.paragraph:
to be safe, secure, especially used of one who securely enjoys prosperity, Job 3:26 12:6 Psa 122:6. Pret. שָׁלַוְתִּי Job loc. cit., fut. יִשְׁלָיוּ. (Arab. سَلاَ to be tranquil and secure in mind. Kindred roots are סָלָה and שָׂלָה, and branches from the same stock are found in the Indo-Germanic languages, both in the sense of quiet and silence, and in that of welfare, as σχόλη, quiet, ease, Lat. sileo=סָלָה, and salv-us, salu-s.)

Derivatives, שָׁלֵו, שָׁלֵיו, שֶׁלֶו, שַׁלְוָה.

II.
